lines: Welcome to tonight. [long pause] Take a breath. [long pause] Let it out however it wants to come out... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] through the mouth is fine. [long pause] You're here now. [long pause] That's the only thing that matters for the next few minutes.

Start by getting comfortable. [long pause] Move your pillow if you need to. [long pause] Adjust the blanket — pull it up to your chin or push it off your feet, whatever your body is asking for. [long pause] Shift your hips, your shoulders, until something settles. [long pause] There's no perfect position. [long pause] Just find the one that says... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] okay. [long pause] This will do.

Notice the places where your body touches the bed. [long pause] Start at the top. [long pause] The back of your head resting on the pillow... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the way the pillow accepts the shape of your skull. [long pause] Your shoulder blades, pressing gently against the mattress... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] you can feel the width of them. [long pause] Your lower back, arched slightly or flat, whatever is natural. [long pause] Your hips... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] heavy, settled, two solid points of weight. [long pause] The backs of your thighs. [long pause] Your calves. [long pause] The heels of your feet, resting at the end of the bed.

These are your anchor points tonight. [long pause] Every time your mind races — and it probably will, because that's what anxious minds do, and there's nothing wrong with that — you can come back to these points. [long pause] They're physical. [long pause] They're real. [long pause] They're not going anywhere. [long pause] The feeling of your head on the pillow. [long pause] The weight of your body on the mattress. [long pause] Start there, and everything else becomes a little quieter.

Let your eyes close if they haven't already. [long pause] Let your jaw soften... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] let your teeth part slightly so there's a sliver of space between them. [long pause] Drop your shoulders away from your ears... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] notice how far they travel. [long pause] Unclench your hands... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] let your fingers uncurl. [long pause] If you were gripping the blanket or pressing your palms together, release that. [long pause] These are the places where the day stores itself in your body, and right now, we're telling it... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the day is over. [long pause] You are off the clock.

Now, your breathing. [long pause] We're going to use a specific pattern that signals safety to your nervous system. [long pause] Breathe in through your nose for a count of four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] one... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four. [long pause] Hold gently at the top — not straining, just pausing... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] one... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three. [long pause] And exhale through your mouth for eight... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] slowly, like you're blowing on a spoonful of hot soup... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] one... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] seven... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] eight.

Again. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four. [long pause] Hold softly... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three. [long pause] And out, letting the breath leave in a long, thin stream... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] seven... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] eight. [long pause] Notice how the body softens on that long exhale. [long pause] That's the parasympathetic nervous system activating. [long pause] That's biology doing what it was designed to do.

Once more. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] hold... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and out, long and slow, all the way to empty. [long pause] Good. [long pause] Let your breath return to its own rhythm now. [long pause] The pattern has been set. [long pause] Your body will remember it even as your attention moves elsewhere.
"Cast all your anxiety on Him, because He cares for you." First Peter, chapter five, verse seven.

Cast is a strong word in the original Greek. [long pause] It doesn't mean place gently. [long pause] It doesn't mean consider transferring. [long pause] It means throw. [long pause] Hurl. [long pause] Get rid of. [long pause] The image is physical, almost violent in its urgency. [long pause] And the reason given is not that God is powerful, though He is. [long pause] The reason is simpler than that. [long pause] He cares for you. [long pause] This is personal, not theological. [long pause] The God of the universe is personally concerned about what kept you awake last night.

Tonight, we're going to practice that casting. [long pause] Not in a forced, positive-thinking kind of way. [long pause] We're going to name the worries honestly, look at them clearly, and then hand them over. [long pause] You may need to do this more than once. [long pause] That's not failure. [long pause] That is the practice itself.

What thought is loudest right now? [long pause] [long pause] Don't go hunting for it... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] it's probably already there, waiting. [long pause] Maybe it's about tomorrow — a meeting, a deadline, a conversation you're dreading. [long pause] Maybe it's something from the past that keeps playing on a loop, reshuffling itself into different angles but never resolving. [long pause] Maybe it's a problem with no clear answer... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] just the formless weight of not knowing what to do.

Name it. [long pause] Silently, in just a few words. [long pause] "I'm worried about... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] " or "I can't stop thinking about... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] " Give it language. [long pause] When you name something, it becomes a thing you can look at, instead of a thing that's looking at you.

Now, let's be honest with this thought. [long pause] Here's the question that actually matters: does this need to be resolved right now? [long pause] [long pause] In the dark? [long pause] [long pause] In your bed? [long pause] [long pause] At this hour?

Has worry ever actually solved a problem while you lay here? [long pause] [long pause] Think about it. [long pause] All those nights you spent rehearsing conversations... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] running scenarios... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] planning for contingencies. [long pause] Did the problem get solved in the bed? [long pause] [long pause] Or did it get solved the next day, in the light, when you could actually do something about it?

Worry rehearses. [long pause] It doesn't resolve. [long pause] It plays the same track over and over, each repetition a little louder, a little more urgent, but nothing changes. [long pause] And meanwhile, it steals the rest your body needs... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the rest your brain needs to actually think clearly tomorrow.
Jesus said it plainly: "Do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself. [long pause] Each day has enough trouble of its own." Matthew six, verse thirty-four.

This is not naivety from someone who lived an easy life. [long pause] This is wisdom from a man who knew betrayal, homelessness, grief, and death. [long pause] He knew what trouble was. [long pause] And he still said... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] tonight is not for carrying tomorrow's weight.

So imagine you're holding this first worry in your hands. [long pause] Not metaphorically... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] actually feel it. [long pause] Give it a shape. [long pause] A weight. [long pause] Maybe it's heavy like a river stone, smooth and cold. [long pause] Maybe it's restless, vibrating, hard to hold still. [long pause] Maybe it's sharp-edged. [long pause] Notice its texture. [long pause] Its temperature. [long pause] How it sits in your palms. [long pause] You're not analyzing it. [long pause] You're just holding it, the way you'd hold something you found on the ground and were deciding what to do with.

Now, slowly... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] lift your hands in your imagination. [long pause] Raise them up. [long pause] And open them. [long pause] Turn your palms over and let the worry fall. [long pause] Not into nothing... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] not into the void... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] but into the hands of the One who said He cares for you. [long pause] See it leave your palms. [long pause] See it received. [long pause] It didn't shatter. [long pause] It didn't disappear. [long pause] It was caught. [long pause] It's being held by someone bigger.
Good. [long pause] Take a breath. [long pause] Feel the lightness in your empty hands.

Now... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] is there another? [long pause] [long pause] There usually is. [long pause] The first worry is rarely alone. [long pause] Let the next one surface. [long pause] Don't chase it... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] just let it rise. [long pause] Something smaller maybe. [long pause] Or something bigger that was hiding behind the first one, waiting until you dealt with that before it showed itself.

Name it. [long pause] Say it silently. [long pause] Hold it in your hands. [long pause] Feel its particular weight... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] its texture... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the specific flavor of unease it carries. [long pause] Every worry feels a little different. [long pause] This one has its own shape.

And when you're ready... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] open your hands again. [long pause] Lift them, and release. [long pause] Let it go. [long pause] It will be there in the morning if you still need it. [long pause] Nothing is lost by setting it down for the night. [long pause] You're not ignoring it. [long pause] You're not pretending it doesn't exist. [long pause] You're choosing not to carry it through the dark hours, when you can do nothing about it anyway.

One more. [long pause] If there's a third thought circling... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] let it come. [long pause] Often the third one is the real one. [long pause] The deep one. [long pause] The one that the others were protecting, like layers around a center.

Whatever it is, name it honestly. [long pause] Even if it feels too big. [long pause] Even if it feels too vague to name. [long pause] "I'm afraid that... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] " or "I don't know how to... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] " or simply "this."

Hold it. [long pause] Feel it fully. [long pause] You're brave enough to look at it. [long pause] And then... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] open your hands one more time. [long pause] Release it into care. [long pause] Into keeping. [long pause] Into the hands that are bigger and steadier than yours.

You've cast three cares tonight. [long pause] Maybe the hands still feel a little heavy. [long pause] Maybe the worries left a residue, the way a cold stone leaves a chill in the palm. [long pause] That's okay. [long pause] Trust grows quietly. [long pause] Not in one dramatic moment of surrender... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] but in these small, repeated acts. [long pause] Cast, and cast again, and cast again. [long pause] The muscle builds over time, and it's building now.
Now we shift. [long pause] The worries have been named and released. [long pause] What's left is the body. [long pause] And the body, freed from the weight of carrying, wants to rest.

Bring your attention to your arms. [long pause] Let them lie wherever they've settled... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] at your sides, on your chest, under the pillow. [long pause] Just notice them.
Silently say to yourself: My right arm is heavy and warm.
My right arm is heavy and warm.

My right arm is heavy... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and warm. [long pause] Feel the weight of it. [long pause] As though it has been filled with warm sand, dense and still. [long pause] Feel the warmth spreading from your shoulder down through your bicep, through the forearm, into the wrist, through the hand, to the tips of your fingers.
Now your left arm. [long pause] My left arm is heavy and warm.
My left arm is heavy and warm.

My left arm is heavy... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and warm. [long pause] Both arms now, sinking into the mattress. [long pause] Dense. [long pause] Warm. [long pause] Still. [long pause] Like they could melt into the bed and you wouldn't mind.
Move to your legs. [long pause] My right leg is heavy and warm.
My right leg is heavy and warm.

Feel the heaviness from your hip to your knee... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] from your knee to your ankle... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] from your ankle to the tips of your toes. [long pause] Like warm sand filling every part of the leg, settling by gravity, compacting gently. [long pause] Your right leg is heavy and warm.
My left leg is heavy and warm.
My left leg is heavy and warm.

Both legs now. [long pause] Heavy. [long pause] Warm. [long pause] Connected to the earth through the mattress. [long pause] Grounded. [long pause] Still. [long pause] You couldn't lift them if you wanted to. [long pause] And you don't want to.
My heartbeat is calm and steady.

My heartbeat is calm... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and steady. [long pause] You don't have to slow it down. [long pause] You don't have to do anything to it. [long pause] Just notice it. [long pause] It's been beating all day without your attention. [long pause] Faithful. [long pause] Consistent. [long pause] A rhythm you didn't choose and don't have to maintain. [long pause] Calm and steady, like a pulse of quiet grace.
My breathing is calm and easy.

My breathing is calm... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and easy. [long pause] It moves in and out without your direction. [long pause] The chest rises on its own. [long pause] The chest falls on its own. [long pause] You can stop managing it. [long pause] You can stop managing everything, just for tonight.
My forehead is cool and smooth.

My forehead is cool... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and smooth. [long pause] As though someone placed a cool cloth across it. [long pause] The furrow between your eyebrows smoothing out. [long pause] The tension in your scalp releasing. [long pause] The muscles of your face softening completely. [long pause] Cool... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] smooth... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] open.
My whole body is heavy and warm and at rest.
My whole body is heavy... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and warm... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and at rest.
My whole body is heavy and warm and at rest.

Lord... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] we have cast our cares on you tonight. [long pause] Not perfectly. [long pause] Not with unwavering faith. [long pause] But honestly. [long pause] Take what we've handed over — the worry, the rehearsal, the fear of what we can't control. [long pause] Hold it while we sleep. [long pause] We don't know how to stop worrying entirely, but we know how to open our hands. [long pause] That's what we did tonight. [long pause] Meet us in the imperfect offering. [long pause] Amen.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]
The cares have been cast. [long pause] The body is warm and heavy. [long pause] The night is quiet around you.

Heavy arms... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] heavy legs... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] calm heart... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] easy breathing... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] cool forehead.
Nothing to solve tonight. [long pause] Nothing to carry. [long pause] The morning will come on its own, and you will meet it rested.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]
Warm... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] heavy... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] quiet... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] released.
